cardiometabolic_tracker v0.2.3 — deployment report

Deployed 2026-06-10 · instance pxbuilder-aomrani.canvasmedical.com · covers v0.2.0→v0.2.3 (commits cd507c1 → 9f9e600) · SDK 0.163.1
pytest: 119 collected · 0 skipped · 119 mocked · 0 live live smoke (v0.2.3): 32/32 Tier-2 cumulative v0.2 live: 95 + 63 + 23 + 32 checks, 0 failures Tier 4: 32/32 (P1, P8 + 3 regression fixtures) Tier 3: never needed
Per build-discipline Gate 4: the pytest suite mocks at the SDK boundary and cannot fail on Canvas runtime behavior — the live Tier-2 browser checks above are the second, mandatory half of every deploy gate. Both are green.

1 · Highlights

  • Adult GLP-1 weight trajectory in one pane. A “Weight Trajectory” button in the Vitals section plots the patient’s weight against their own baseline with % total body weight loss, in a large right chart pane beside the note.
  • Milestones that respect the chart. 5/10/15% TBWL reference lines render only when they fall inside the plotted range, and color by whether the patient has crossed them.
  • An evidence band that picks itself. The expected-response corridor is selected from the patient’s active GLP-1 medication (semaglutide→STEP-1, tirzepatide→SURMOUNT-1, liraglutide→SCALE), with a safe STEP-1 fallback when no medication is on file.
  • Velocity and flags, never directives. A 4-week velocity stat plus informational plateau / regain / rapid-loss badges with descriptive copy.
  • v0.2.3: provenance where the clinician’s eyes are. SCALE’s synthesized bounds now say so in the legend (“SCALE, estimated”), and every band carries an ⓘ panel with the trial name, outcome summary, and NEJM citation — SCALE’s adds an amber “illustrative, not statistical” note.

2 · Screenshots

3 · Bugs found & fixed

FoundIssueFix
v0.2.1Mixing FHIR-created (tz-aware) and UI-created (naive) observation dates crashed four comparison sites with TypeError; one site was found only by a pipeline-level test. Module-level _strip_tz as the key/operand for every date sort/min/delta; 3 regression tests.
v0.2.1Fixed 760px container clipped the right-aligned flag badges off-screen in narrow (575px) chart panes — flag explanations looked missing. Fluid container (max-width) + flex-wrap on the stats bar; verified at 575px.
v0.2.2Agent detection ran one codings query per medication (N+1). prefetch_related("codings"); pinned by a mock-chain assertion.
v0.2.2Every chart layer re-ran the scaffold, rebuilding scales/axes/gridlines 4–5× per open. Layers take setData(); one scale pass after all data lands. DOM proven identical live.
seedingCanvas schema rejections: Patient create without the us-core-birthsex extension (HTTP 500); weight unit string lbs (HTTP 422 — must be lb). Guard-protected aborts caught both before any stray writes; seeder fixed and re-run.

4 · Observations & architectural findings

  • FHIR-created MedicationStatements surface in the SDK Medication model — Medication.objects.for_patient(id).active() sees them (basis of agent detection).
  • The LaunchModalEffect target does not change the iframe context: modal and right-chart-pane both render plugin content in about:srcdoc, so browser-test selectors survive presentation changes.
  • Plugin data access is fully server-side: the only browser-visible signal per chart open is a single GraphQL render POST — network assertions must not expect FHIR calls.
  • FHIR writes to this sandbox are permanent (DELETE/PUT/PATCH/enter_in_error all blocked) — every write tool carries a created-this-run + name read-back guard. Patient renames (PUT) are the one allowed mutation, used to give demo patients readable names with scenario chart notes.
  • The Canvas host page emits console noise (CSP report-only warning, analytics failures) on every page; console assertions must classify entries by origin frame and fail only on plugin-attributable errors — now a required step in the debug-capture console playbook.

5 · Future version considerations

  • Reference-table reconciliation: the science reference’s SURMOUNT-1 (22.5% @ wk 72) and SCALE (8.4% @ wk 56) rows differ from published primary-endpoint means (−20.9% by dose; −8.0%/8.4 kg) — likely estimand and kg-vs-% mixups. Band geometry was deliberately left unchanged in v0.2.3; reconcile in v0.3.
  • Published SCALE percentiles to replace the synthesized 0.5×–1.5× bounds.
  • Mid-series medication changes: which band (or both) when the agent switches.
  • Milestone crossed semantics on regain: historically-crossed-then-lost thresholds currently render uncrossed (latest-TBWL rule) — decide styling.
  • Deferred from v0.2 scope: dose-titration markers, week-12 non-responder banner, BMI transition markers, trend extrapolation.

6 · Tooling & skill notes

  • Making chart layer state introspectable (top-level layer objects readable from frame.evaluate) turned visual checks into exact numeric assertions — the single highest-leverage testing decision of the build.
  • Browser checks are driven by the seeding manifest, so live assertions and seeded data cannot drift.
  • Selector-stable assertion scripts turn “the DOM must be identical after this refactor” into a 3-minute check (used to pin the v0.2.2 scaffold restructure).
  • New skills installed this cycle: build-discipline (session-start gates; its Gate 1 caught the en-dash/hyphen citation drift before hardcoding) and deploy-report (this artifact). The debug-capture console playbook gained the plugin-attribution step, closing the v0.2 friction item.
  • CLI friction: canvas logs requires --host and streams indefinitely — use the documented background-kill pattern.
